title: SSO login redirect loop after Okta-to-Entra ID migration due to stale session cookies
problem: After migrating SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users experience an infinite redirect loop during login. They authenticate successfully at Microsoft but are immediately redirected back to the identity provider upon returning to TaskFlow. The remaining 70% of users are unaffected. Incognito mode and clearing cookies sometimes resolve the issue temporarily.
root_cause_hypothesis: Stale session or authentication cookies from the old Okta SSO configuration are interfering with the new Entra ID flow. DevTools shows a Set-Cookie header in the response from Microsoft's side that does not persist to the next request, suggesting either (a) an existing cookie with the same name/domain from the Okta era is taking precedence, (b) a SameSite or Secure attribute mismatch is causing the browser to reject the new cookie, or (c) the cookie domain/path from the old configuration doesn't match the new one, so the old cookie is sent instead. The 30/70 split likely corresponds to users who had persistent Okta session cookies versus those who didn't (new devices, cleared cookies recently, etc.).
reproduction_steps: Identify an affected user (one who has been using TaskFlow since before the Okta-to-Entra ID migration)
Have them navigate to the TaskFlow login page in a browser with existing cookies
Observe: user authenticates at Microsoft Entra ID successfully
Observe: user is redirected back to TaskFlow but immediately redirected to Microsoft again, creating an infinite loop
Verify: the same user can log in via incognito/private window or after clearing all cookies
environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1, self-hosted deployment. Multiple browsers affected (Chrome, Edge, Firefox). Not browser-specific — same users fail across browsers, and unaffected users succeed on the same browsers.
impact: Approximately 30% of the team cannot log into TaskFlow reliably. Workarounds (incognito, clearing cookies) are temporary and disruptive. This blocks daily work for affected users.
recommended_fix: 1. Inspect the TaskFlow authentication cookie configuration — check cookie names, domain, path, SameSite, and Secure attributes. Compare against what the old Okta integration set. 2. Look for leftover Okta-related cookies (e.g., session cookies with the old issuer or nonce values) that may conflict with the Entra ID flow. 3. Consider adding a migration step that explicitly clears or invalidates old SSO session cookies on first login attempt post-migration. 4. Verify the session middleware is correctly processing the new Entra ID tokens and not rejecting them due to stale issuer/audience validation config. 5. Check server logs for token validation errors or session creation failures during the redirect cycle.
proposed_test_case: Simulate the migration scenario: create a session cookie matching the old Okta SSO cookie format, then attempt to authenticate via the Entra ID flow. Verify that the new authentication completes successfully and the old cookie is replaced rather than causing a redirect loop.
information_gaps: Server-side logs during a failed login attempt (reporter is working on obtaining these)
Exact Node.js version and authentication framework/library used by TaskFlow
Whether all affected users definitively predate the SSO migration (confirming the stale-cookie hypothesis)
Specific cookie names and domains set by the old Okta integration versus the new Entra ID integration
